ksa_text: Knowledge of model risk management regulatory requirements including Federal Reserve SR 11-7, OCC guidance, and supervisory expectations for model governance and independent validation.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of test procedures, principles, and methodologies (e.g., Capabilities and Maturity Model Integration (CMMI)).

eu_ai_act.top_element_label: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of appropriate use policies and procedures

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA RM-K-009 (Risk Management/Knowledge) scores 0.434 composite (highest: NICE at 0.434). Scope is edge_case for Clinical AI Specialist: 1 in-scope signals, 0 out-of-scope signals. Seniority is appropriate.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with edge_case scope, Risk Management, composite 0.434. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support Risk Management for Clinical AI Specialist at this seniority?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Risk Management appears in ~40% of Clinical AI Specialist postings as a secondary competency. Clinical risk management overlaps with AI safety and post-market surveillance.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Include upheld. Risk Management with role-relevant signals (1) is market-supported. Final decision: include.
